home.title: AUROSY Skill Factory
home.lead: Local web UI for the REST API (<c1></c1>) and WebSocket telemetry. Start the backend: see <c2></c2>.
home.leadShort: Control center: REST (<c1></c1>) and WebSocket telemetry. Run instructions — in the repo README.
home.threeTiersLead: Matches the Python tools on the robot SDK (Pose Studio, skill export, Scenario Studio). The web app wires APIs, JSON, and handoff to disk paths on the dev machine.
home.tier1: Action — Motion Studio (3D pose & sliders), Authoring (keyframes JSON), Pipeline (validate → preprocess → playback → train).
home.tier2: Skill (trained) — Jobs runs async train on the server; Packages holds the Skill Bundle (.tar.gz) — the deployable “skill” artifact in this stack.
home.tier3: Scenario — Skill Studio chains catalog actions; target clip ~{{target}} s (guide {{min}}–{{max}} s). Export scenario.json (version 1) for high_level_motions/ on the SDK tree.

shortcut.authoringDesc: Keyframes, motion, scenario — schemas and server validation.
shortcut.poseDesc: 3D MuJoCo and joint sliders by group.
shortcut.jobsDesc: Async train queue — turn a trajectory into a trained skill (Phase 5).
shortcut.packagesDesc: Skill Bundles — download or publish trained skill archives.
shortcut.helpDesc: FAQ on API, Skill Studio, and pipeline.

home.trainPathsBody: <strong1>Pipeline</strong1> (<lp>open</lp>): fast synchronous CLI on the server — <c1>POST /api/pipeline/train</c1>, result in the response. <strong2>Platform</strong2> (<lj>Jobs</lj>): async queue — <c2>POST /api/jobs/train</c2>, workspace isolation and concurrency limits on the backend; after success, Skill Bundle under <lpk>Packages</lpk>. More in <lh>Help</lh>.
home.draftsPlaceholder: Recent drafts will appear here when client or server persistence is added.
home.bulletAuthoring: Authoring — keyframes / motion / scenario (Phase 0), JSON Schema + server validation.
home.bulletTelemetry: WebSocket — mock joint stream from the backend (for integrations; see OpenAPI).
home.bulletScenarios: Skill Studio — mid-level action list and duration estimate (like Scenario Studio).
home.bulletPipeline: Pipeline — validate/preprocess/playback/train on the server; bridge from action JSON to a train-ready trajectory.
home.bulletPose: Motion Studio — browser MuJoCo and joint sliders; export pose.json and server drafts (Phase 5).

settings.title: Settings
settings.apiBaseSameOrigin: Requests use the same origin as the page (in dev, Vite proxies when <c1></c1> is unset).
settings.languageHint: Applies to labels and messages across the app.
settings.docsUi: UI architecture, design system, roadmap: docs/g1-control-ui/ in the monorepo.
settings.docsWeb: Backend pairing and env: web/README.md, web/frontend/README.md.
settings.docsFaq: Full help: <link>Help</link> page (<c1>/help</c1>) and <c2>docs/g1-control-ui/FAQ.md</c2> in the monorepo.
settings.backendMetaHint: Snippet from GET /api/meta when the backend is up:
settings.platformUserHint: For Phase 5 the client sends the <c1></c1> header on /api/platform/*, /api/jobs*, and /api/packages*. This is not full sign-in — only the owner id for the backend. Default: <c2></c2> at build time or built-in fallback (see README).
settings.localDevTitle: Run API and simulator on your machine
settings.localDevLead: The web UI needs a separate API server (FastAPI on port 8000). That is not the same process as the MuJoCo window. Start the API first; open the simulator in another terminal if you need it.
settings.localDevTelemetryNote: Browser telemetry today comes from the API as a mock stream. The simulator window is for physics and DDS; feeding sim joint angles into the web UI needs backend integration—it cannot be done with a browser button alone.
settings.localDevHint: Paste in Terminal from the platform repo root (folders web/ and unitree_mujoco/). First-time API setup: see web/README.md — venv and pip install -r requirements.txt.

backendBanner.title: Backend unavailable.
backendBanner.detail: Could not reach the API (check <c1></c1>). Make sure the FastAPI server is running — see the platform backend README.

authoring.title: Authoring Phase 0
authoring.lead: JSON editor: degrees and seconds for keyframes; validation with JSON Schema (client) and <c1></c1> (server).
authoring.schemaBadgesAria: schema_version from contract file and from JSON
authoring.copyPathAria: Copy JSON Schema instance path to clipboard
authoring.versionMismatch: JSON has <c1></c1> = "{{payload}}", schema file has "{{expected}}". Server validation still works; sync contracts when updating the backend.
authoring.prefillFromPoseOk: Keyframes were filled from Motion Studio.
authoring.prefillFromPoseErr: Could not apply keyframes from Motion Studio.

telemetry.title: Telemetry
telemetry.lead: Monitoring: joint angles over WebSocket <c1></c1> (mock or DDS bridge). Groups and labels from <c2></c2>. Unlike Motion Studio, there is no robot diagram — only table or sliders.
telemetry.ddsModeBanner: GET /api/meta reports dds telemetry (often via G1_USE_DDS_TELEMETRY on the backend). Until a DDS bridge is deployed, /ws/telemetry may close immediately or reconnect in a loop — use mock telemetry on the server for typical UI development (see backend README/OpenAPI). More: Help.
telemetry.ddsUnstableHint: The connection is not staying up (WebSocket close code: {{code}}). With DDS mode and no working bridge, switch the backend to mock or check the network and WebSocket proxy.

scenario.title: Skill Studio (like Scenario Studio)
scenario.lead: Actions from . Duration estimate is server-side; target clip window around {{target}} s (guide {{min}}–{{max}} s).
scenario.catalogEmpty: Catalog is empty. Check mid-level paths on the backend.
scenario.emptyCatalogDesc: Check mid-level paths on the backend and the GET /api/mid-level/actions response.
scenario.emptyChainDesc: Pick actions in the table above and use “Add to scenario”.
scenario.estimateNeedNode: Add at least one node to the scenario.
scenario.emptyChain: No nodes — add from the table above.
scenario.estimateDebounceHint: After edits, duration re-estimates automatically (~{{ms}} ms).
scenario.warnDuration: Total estimate is outside the target window {{min}}–{{max}} s (guide ~{{target}} s). Adjust nodes or review the scenario intent.
scenario.exportNeedNodes: Add at least one action to the chain before export.
scenario.scenarioJsonHint: File format matches Python Scenario Studio: version 1, title, nodes (subdir, action_name, speed, repeat). Save under high_level_motions/<name>/ on the SDK repo.

pipeline.title: CLI pipeline
pipeline.lead: Wrappers around <c1></c1>, <c2></c2>, <c3></c3>. PYTHONPATH is set on the server. Full Phase 0 editor — <link>Authoring</link>.
pipeline.cliWarn: CLI tools not found in <c1></c1> (see below). The backend may use <c2></c2> — check OpenAPI/README in the backend repo.
pipeline.cliInPath: CLI in PATH: preprocess={{pre}}, playback={{pb}}, train={{tr}} (otherwise <c1></c1>)
pipeline.phase0Lead: Same JSON as for preprocess below. Run before the first preprocess if possible.
pipeline.validateWarn: Last server validation of keyframes reported errors. You can still run preprocess; verify the data.
pipeline.playbackLead: If the reference path is empty, preprocess runs from the field above, then playback with <c1></c1>.
pipeline.iaRegionAria: About pipeline train vs platform job queue
pipeline.trainPathsInfo: Pipeline here runs preprocess / playback and optionally synchronous train via subprocess (POST /api/pipeline/train) — the response returns in the same request. For async training in an isolated workspace and Skill Bundle packaging, use Jobs (POST /api/jobs/train); concurrency limits are enforced on the backend.
pipeline.actionReadyHandoff: When preprocess succeeds, the motion is ready for training: use «Continue to train queue» or place outputs next to <c1>mid_level_motions/</c1> on the SDK machine (see <lh>Help → SDK paths</lh>).
pipeline.prefillKeyframesOk: Keyframes field updated from Motion Studio.
pipeline.trainSyncLead: Synchronous subprocess on the server (<c1>POST /api/pipeline/train</c1>). This is not the Jobs queue — for async train open <lj>Jobs</lj>.
pipeline.preprocessCtaLead: You can send <c1>reference_trajectory</c1> to the queue form — you still press «Enqueue» on Jobs (no automatic double start).
pipeline.preprocessRefParseErr: Could not parse reference_trajectory_json for transfer to Jobs.
pipeline.errTrainPaths: Provide a path to reference_trajectory.json and/or config (e.g. smoke_train_config.json).

jobs.title: Jobs & platform (Phase 5)
jobs.lead: Skill tier: store JSON artifacts, enqueue async train, and watch logs — this is how a trajectory becomes a trained model in the platform. Requires routes and a consistent (see Settings).
jobs.workerHint: The queue is processed by a background worker on the backend. If jobs stay queued, check <c1>G1_PLATFORM_WORKER_ENABLED</c1> and the backend docs.
jobs.sectionArtifactLead: POST /api/platform/artifacts/… — name in the URL path; body is JSON (e.g. reference_trajectory from preprocess). The server validates the name.
jobs.errArtifactJson: Invalid JSON for the artifact body.
jobs.sectionTrainLead: POST /api/jobs/train — exactly one reference source: inline JSON or a saved artifact name. Demonstration is optional.
jobs.prefilledFromPipeline: Reference field was filled from Pipeline (preprocess). Review the JSON and options, then press «Enqueue».
jobs.errRefBoth: Choose only one reference source: JSON or artifact name.
jobs.errRefNone: Provide a reference: JSON or artifact name.
jobs.errDemoBoth: For demonstration, send either JSON or an artifact name, not both.
jobs.emptyListDesc: After you enqueue train, jobs appear here. Ensure the backend worker is enabled.
jobs.detailLead: Status and log tails refresh automatically until the job finishes.
jobs.noLogsYet: Log tails are empty or not yet available.
jobs.bundleLead: POST /api/packages/from-job/{job_id} — after a successful train, build a Skill Bundle (the packaged skill) for the Packages screen.

packages.title: Packages (Skill Bundle)
packages.lead: Skill tier: list, download, upload, and publish Skill Bundles (/api/packages) — platform packaging of a trained skill; distinct from on-disk library/skills/ folders in the SDK repo. Uploads must be .tar.gz. Use the same X-User-Id as for Jobs (Settings).
packages.sectionUploadLead: POST /api/packages/upload — .tar.gz only (multipart field is usually file; confirm in backend OpenAPI).
packages.emptyDesc: After a successful train, create a bundle from the job detail page or upload a .tar.gz here.
packages.jobsHint: Create a package from a successful job on the <l1>Jobs</l1> screen.

pose.title: Motion Studio
pose.lead: Here you can easily configure and save poses and create movements.
pose.placeholderBanner: The diagram under <c1></c1> is a placeholder until §1.4 assets from <c2></c1> (backend repo) are copied in.
pose.wasmSlidersHint: After the scene loads, use sliders or the numeric field; angles are in degrees.
pose.saveDraftNoPose: No complete joint set to save.

robotDiagram.svgAria: Body zones by joint groups from API
robotDiagram.svgTitle: Click a zone to highlight a joint group
robotDiagram.hint: Zones are built from <c1></c1>. Replace <c2></c2> with assets from backend guide §1.4; add custom regions in <c3></c3> if needed.

faq.title: Help & FAQ
faq.lead: Short answers for AUROSY Skill Factory. The same content lives in docs/g1-control-ui/FAQ.md in the monorepo.

api.q1: How do I know the API is reachable?
api.a1: Home shows “API reachable”. On failure, a banner appears with Retry for GET /api/health.
api.a2: Build-time env var VITE_API_BASE sets the base URL for REST and WebSocket. When unset, requests use the page origin. In dev, Vite proxies /api and /ws to FastAPI (see vite.config.ts). The effective value in a production build is shown under Settings.
api.q3: Why is the UI on port 5173 but the API is “8000”?
api.a3: The browser uses relative paths; the dev server forwards them to the backend. Do not hand-roll URLs — use <c1>src/api/client.ts</c1>.
api.a4: Routes /api/platform/*, /api/jobs*, and /api/packages* expect the X-User-Id header as the resource owner id. Settings shows the effective value; browser override, VITE_PLATFORM_USER_ID, or fallback — see the frontend README. For Phase 5 calls use apiFetch from the client. This is not full authentication.

authoring.q1: What do the Authoring tabs do?
authoring.a1: They edit the three Phase 0 artifacts as JSON. Golden fixtures live under <c1>public/fixtures/golden/v1/</c1>; validation uses the copied schemas.
authoring.q2: How is “JSON Schema (client)” different from “server Phase 0”?
authoring.a2: Client validation uses the local schema copy in the browser (AJV). Server validation is POST /api/validate — the backend source of truth.
authoring.q3: Why can client and server disagree on the same file?
authoring.a3: Stale files under <c1>public/contracts/</c1> or a different <c2>schema_version</c2> in the JSON. After backend updates, copy the latest <c3>*.schema.json</c3> and rebuild the frontend.

pose.q1: Why is there a placeholder banner?
pose.a1: Until §1.4 assets from <c1>frontend_developer_guide</c1> (backend repo) are installed, the UI may show a placeholder — see <c2>POSE_ASSETS_ARE_PLACEHOLDER</c2> in code.
pose.q2: How do I clear the warning?
pose.a2: Install final assets under <c1>public/pose/</c1>, adjust <c2>pose-overlay.json</c2> if needed, then disable placeholder mode in <c3>web/frontend/src/lib/poseAssets.ts</c3> (see file comments).
pose.q3: How does 3D MuJoCo in the browser work?
pose.a3: Rendering uses <c1>@mujoco/mujoco</c1> (WASM) and MJCF from <c2>mujoco_menagerie/unitree_g1</c2> — a kinematic pose preview (no physics step). Exported angles are Phase 0–compatible. Run <c3>npm run fetch:menagerie-g1</c3> once in the frontend folder before first use.
pose.q4: Where does “Save pose to server” write?
pose.a4: <c1>POST /api/platform/pose-drafts</c1> (Phase 5, <c2>X-User-Id</c2> header) stores JSON under the user’s <c3>pose_drafts</c3> directory on the platform data disk. Requires a running backend with Phase 5.

scenario.q1: What does the 25–35 s warning mean?
scenario.a1: The total from <c1>POST /api/scenario/estimate</c1> is outside the guide window (~30 s target). It is a hint, not a hard block; heuristics live on the server.

pipeline.q1: Why does the UI show detect-cli?
pipeline.a1: The backend checks whether preprocess / playback / train are on <c1>PATH</c1>. If not, it may fall back to <c2>python -m …</c2> — see backend OpenAPI/README.
pipeline.q2: Where do I read preprocess or playback errors?
pipeline.a2: In the Pipeline output blocks: exit_code, stdout, stderr; long JSON can be expanded with “Show full”.
pipeline.q3: Can I run preprocess if validate failed?
pipeline.a3: The UI warns but does not block — data quality is on the operator.

deployment.q1: How is CORS different in dev vs production?
deployment.a1: Development backends often use <c1>allow_origins=["*"]</c1>. In production, narrow origins to trusted UI hosts. A robust pattern is same-origin SPA with reverse-proxy for <c2>/api</c2> and <c3>/ws</c3> so the browser does not rely on cross-origin CORS for the API.
deployment.q2: Do I need cookies or credentials in the frontend?
deployment.a2: The client uses <c1>fetch</c1> without <c2>credentials: 'include'</c2>. Phase 5 uses the <c3>X-User-Id</c3> header, not cookie sessions. If you later enable cookie auth on the backend, you may need to adjust <c1>apiFetch</c1> in <c2>src/api/client.ts</c2> and CORS for credentials — follow the backend README/OpenAPI.
deployment.q3: Jobs stay queued in production — what should I check?
deployment.a3: The background worker must be running (often <c1>G1_PLATFORM_WORKER_ENABLED</c1>). Check server logs and environment for the API process. See also the platform FAQ entries.
deployment.q4: How do I set the API base URL for a built app?
deployment.a4: Set VITE_API_BASE at build time to the API URL without a trailing slash, or leave it empty for same-origin behind a reverse-proxy. WebSocket URLs follow the same host/scheme. The effective value appears in Settings.
deployment.q5: Where is the full deployment checklist?
deployment.a5: In the repo: <c1>docs/g1-control-ui/DEPLOYMENT.md</c1> — a concise checklist (proxy, WS, worker, telemetry, Phase 5, CORS).

platform.h2: Platform: artifacts, train queue, and packages
platform.a1: Large JSON (e.g. reference_trajectory) is easier to store on the platform by name and reference from POST /api/jobs/train. The Jobs screen (/jobs) saves JSON via Phase 5; exact paths are in the backend OpenAPI.
platform.q2: How is Pipeline train different from a queued job?
platform.a2: Pipeline runs train as a synchronous subprocess (POST /api/pipeline/train) with the result in the response. Jobs is an async queue (POST /api/jobs/train) with workspace isolation and concurrency limits on the backend; status and logs are on /jobs.
platform.q3: My job stays queued — what should I check?
platform.a3: The backend queue worker must be running (often <c1>G1_PLATFORM_WORKER_ENABLED</c1>). Without it, jobs do not progress. Also ensure <c2>X-User-Id</c2> matches the one used when saving artifacts (Settings / <c3>VITE_PLATFORM_USER_ID</c3>).
platform.q4: How do I get a Skill Bundle after train?
platform.a4: When a job has succeeded, open its detail page and use «Create Skill Bundle» (POST /api/packages/from-job/…), or call the API from OpenAPI. List and download live under Packages (/packages).
platform.q5: Why can’t I publish a package?
platform.a5: The backend may enforce a validation gate before publish. A <c1>409</c1> from <c2>PATCH /api/packages/…</c2> includes the reason in the response body (e.g. <c3>failure_reasons</c3>) — the UI shows the server text. Skipping the gate is a server-side dev setting only, not something the UI configures.
platform.q6: How do I move reference from Pipeline into the train queue?
platform.a6: After a successful preprocess on Pipeline, click «Continue to train queue» — /jobs opens with inline reference_trajectory prefilled. Enqueue does not run automatically: press «Enqueue» after review. Alternatively save the JSON as a platform artifact and use its name in the train form.
platform.q7: Can a job time out on the server?
platform.a7: Yes: backends usually enforce a maximum job duration (often a setting like job_timeout_sec). Long train runs may fail with a timeout — confirm the exact name and values in the backend README/OpenAPI. If GET /api/meta returns job_timeout_sec or platform_worker_enabled, they may appear in the metadata block on Settings.

skillfactory.q1: How does the web UI relate to the Python tools?
skillfactory.a1: The same three levels: action (keyframes + pipeline / mid-level folders), skill (train → Skill Bundle or SDK library/skills/), scenario (Skill Studio + scenario.json v1). The SPA orchestrates HTTP/WebSocket; files on disk live in the SDK repo. See docs/g1-control-ui/platform_web_mapping.md in this monorepo.
skillfactory.q2: Where do scenario.json and actions live on disk?
skillfactory.a2: Typical SDK layout (names may vary): <c1>mid_level_motions/basic_actions|complex_actions/<name>/</c1> (pose.json, execute.py), <c2>high_level_motions/<name>/scenario.json</c2>, <c3>library/skills/<name>/</c3> for generated skills. Export from Skill Studio and place files there for Scenario Studio CLI.

i18n.q1: How do I switch Russian and English?
i18n.a1: Use the RU / EN toggle in the sidebar and on Settings. The choice persists; the document lang attribute updates.
